activities_projects.title: Diary Entry of Lencho
activities_projects.objective: Understand Lencho’s feelings and thoughts after the hailstorm.
steps: Imagine you are Lencho after the storm
Write a diary entry describing your emotions and worries
Include at least three events from the story
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character emotions and express them creatively.
activities_projects.evaluation: Emotional depth, connection with story events, and correct language usage

activities_projects.title: Postmaster’s Perspective Letter
activities_projects.objective: Explore the story from another character’s viewpoint.
steps: Imagine you are the postmaster
Write a letter to a friend describing Lencho’s faith and your decision to help him
Mention your feelings about Lencho’s reaction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand multiple perspectives in a story.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of viewpoint, creativity, and relevance

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify relationships between events in the story.
steps: List major events (hailstorm, letter to God, money received)
Write their causes and effects
Present the chart in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop analytical thinking skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of cause-effect connections and presentation clarity

activities_projects.title: Theme Exploration Poster
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central themes of faith and human kindness.
steps: Identify two main themes
Write examples from the story supporting each theme
Illustrate with simple drawings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect themes with textual evidence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct theme identification and supporting examples

activities_projects.title: Debate: Was Lencho Right?
activities_projects.objective: Develop critical thinking and speaking skills.
steps: Divide class into two groups (For and Against Lencho’s reaction)
Prepare 3 points supporting your side
Conduct a short debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will evaluate character behavior logically.

activities_projects.title: Newspaper Report on the Hailstorm
steps: Imagine you are a reporter
Write a short news report about the hailstorm
Include headline, date, and details
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will practice formal writing style.

activities_projects.title: Character Comparison Chart
steps: Draw two columns
Write qualities of Lencho in one and Postmaster in the other
Add examples from the text

activities_projects.title: Letter to Lencho
steps: Imagine you know the truth about the money
Write a polite letter to Lencho explaining who helped him
Suggest how he should respond
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will practice persuasive and explanatory writing.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Context Usage
steps: Choose 8 new words from the chapter
Write their meanings
Use them in short meaningful paragraphs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will apply vocabulary in context.

activities_projects.title: Alternate Ending Creation
steps: Imagine Lencho discovers the truth
Write a new ending in 150 words
Share with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance creative writing and imagination skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, coherence, and connection to original story
